annotations.label: Complex number

anchor.snippet: A complex number is an expression of the form a + bi

annotations.note: The structure `Complex` is defined with two real fields `re` and `im`.

annotations.label: Real and imaginary parts

anchor.snippet: the real number a is called its real part

annotations.note: Real/imaginary parts are the structure projections `Complex.re` and `Complex.im`.

annotations.label: Complex plane (Argand diagram)

anchor.snippet: which is then called the complex plane or Argand diagram

annotations.note: Mathlib has the bijection `Complex.equivRealProd : ℂ ≃ ℝ × ℝ` but no named 'complex plane' definition.

annotations.label: Purely imaginary number

anchor.snippet: A purely imaginary number bi is a complex number

annotations.note: No dedicated 'purely imaginary' predicate; expressed via `z.re = 0`.

annotations.label: Addition of complex numbers

anchor.snippet: are added by separately adding their real and imaginary parts

annotations.note: Addition is defined componentwise via the `Add ℂ` instance at line 164 of `Basic.lean`.

annotations.label: Parallelogram rule for addition

anchor.snippet: The addition can be geometrically visualized as follows

annotations.note: The geometric parallelogram visualization for complex addition is not stated as a lemma.

annotations.label: Multiplication of complex numbers

anchor.snippet: The product of two complex numbers is computed as follows

annotations.note: Multiplication is defined via the `Mul ℂ` instance at line 206 of `Basic.lean`.

annotations.label: Sample product

anchor.snippet: For example,

annotations.note: Illustrative numeric example, no corresponding Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: Complex numbers form a field

anchor.snippet: the complex numbers form an algebraic structure known as a field

annotations.note: The `Field ℂ` instance is established at line 678 of `Basic.lean`.

annotations.label: Complex conjugate

anchor.section: Complex conjugate, absolute value, argument and division
anchor.snippet: The complex conjugate of the complex number

annotations.note: Conjugation is the `star` operation on ℂ, available as `conj` in the `ComplexConjugate` scope.

annotations.label: Conjugation is an involution

anchor.section: Complex conjugate, absolute value, argument and division
anchor.snippet: Conjugating twice gives the original complex number

annotations.note: Aliased as `Complex.conj_conj := starRingEnd_self_apply`.

annotations.label: Real iff equals conjugate

anchor.section: Complex conjugate, absolute value, argument and division
anchor.snippet: A complex number is real if and only if it equals its own conjugate

annotations.note: Stated at line 467 of `Basic.lean` as `conj z = z ↔ ∃ r : ℝ, z = r`.

annotations.label: Absolute value (modulus)

anchor.section: Complex conjugate, absolute value, argument and division
anchor.snippet: This allows to define the absolute value

annotations.note: Modulus is the norm: `‖z‖ = √(normSq z)`, via the `NormedField ℂ` instance.

anchors.section: Complex conjugate, absolute value, argument and division
anchors.snippet: This allows to define the absolute value

annotations.label: Modulus as distance

anchor.section: Complex conjugate, absolute value, argument and division
anchor.snippet: is the distance from the origin to the point representing the complex number

annotations.note: `dist z w = ‖z - w‖` in ℂ; specialising to w = 0 gives the distance from origin.

anchors.section: Complex conjugate, absolute value, argument and division
anchors.snippet: is the distance from the origin to the point representing the complex number

annotations.label: Reciprocal of a complex number

anchor.section: Complex conjugate, absolute value, argument and division
anchor.snippet: Using the conjugate, the reciprocal of a nonzero complex number

annotations.note: `z⁻¹ = conj z * (normSq z)⁻¹` is proved at line 648 of `Basic.lean`.

annotations.label: Division of complex numbers

anchor.section: Complex conjugate, absolute value, argument and division
anchor.snippet: the division of an arbitrary complex number

annotations.note: Division is provided by the `Field ℂ` instance, giving `w / z = w * z⁻¹`.

anchors.section: Complex conjugate, absolute value, argument and division
anchors.snippet: the division of an arbitrary complex number

annotations.label: Argument of a complex number

anchor.section: Complex conjugate, absolute value, argument and division
anchor.snippet: The argument of z (sometimes called the "phase"

annotations.note: `Complex.arg : ℂ → ℝ` is defined at line 30 of `Arg.lean`.

annotations.label: Principal value of argument

anchor.section: Complex conjugate, absolute value, argument and division
anchor.snippet: which is referred to as the principal value

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Complex.arg` is the principal value: `arg_mem_Ioc` shows `arg z ∈ Set.Ioc (-π) π`.

annotations.label: Polar form

anchor.snippet: This identity is referred to as the polar form of

annotations.note: `‖x‖ * (cos (arg x) + sin (arg x) * I) = x` is the polar form identity.

anchor.snippet: the absolute values are multiplied and the arguments are added

annotations.note: Mathlib has `arg_mul_eq_add_arg_iff` and `norm_mul`, but the combined statement of the multiplicative polar law is split across lemmas.

annotations.label: de Moivre's formula

anchor.snippet: The n -th power of a complex number can be computed using de Moivre's formula

annotations.note: `(cos θ + sin θ * I)^n` formula at line 539 of `Trigonometric.lean`.

anchor.snippet: The n n th roots of a complex number

annotations.note: General `Polynomial.nthRoots` exists and roots-of-unity in ℂ are formalized, but no single named lemma listing the n distinct n-th roots of an arbitrary complex number.

annotations.label: C is algebraically closed

anchor.snippet: is called an algebraically closed field

annotations.note: The `IsAlgClosed ℂ` instance is registered at line 50 of `Polynomial/Basic.lean`.

annotations.label: Quadratic extension definition of C

anchor.snippet: One formal definition of the complex numbers is that they form the quadratic extension field

annotations.note: Mathlib defines ℂ concretely as ℝ² rather than as a quadratic extension ℝ[X]/(X²+1).

annotations.label: Polynomial ring R[X]

anchor.snippet: forms a commutative ring , called the polynomial ring

annotations.note: `structure Polynomial (R : Type*) [Semiring R]` is defined at line 71 of `Polynomial/Basic.lean`.

anchors.snippet: forms a commutative ring , called the polynomial ring

annotations.label: Quotient ring isomorphism

anchor.snippet: Basic facts of abstract algebra then imply that the kernel of the above map is an ideal

annotations.note: First isomorphism theorem for rings is in Mathlib, but no specific instance constructing ℂ as ℝ[X]/(X²+1).

annotations.label: Matrix representation of complex numbers

anchor.snippet: Complex numbers a + bi can also be represented by 2 × 2 matrices

annotations.note: No 2×2 real-matrix representation of ℂ is defined in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: Complex numbers a + bi can also be represented by 2 × 2 matrices

annotations.label: Ring isomorphism to matrix field

anchor.snippet: is a ring isomorphism from the field of complex numbers to the ring of these matrices

annotations.note: No corresponding ring isomorphism ℂ ≃+* (matrices) is formalized.

anchors.snippet: is a ring isomorphism from the field of complex numbers to the ring of these matrices

annotations.label: Holomorphic function

anchor.snippet: is called holomorphic or complex differentiable at a point

annotations.note: Holomorphic means `DifferentiableAt ℂ f x`; no dedicated `Holomorphic` predicate, but the concept is captured.

annotations.label: Real-differentiable but not complex-differentiable

anchor.snippet: is differentiable as a function

annotations.note: The explicit example (e.g. conjugation) is not formalized as a lemma.

anchor.snippet: A real differentiable function is complex differentiable if and only if it satisfies the Cauchy–Riemann equations

annotations.note: The `CauchyRiemann` section in `Analysis/Complex/Conformal.lean` proves the equivalence.

annotations.label: Algebraic numbers
